preview.summary: Asia's top-ranked side meets a Tunisia team that conceded zero goals in qualifying, a clash of Japan's slick attacking movement against North African discipline. Both will view this as a pivotal Group F fixture for the knockout race.
preview.homeForm: Tunisia qualified unbeaten from CAF Group H without conceding a single goal, sitting 44th in the FIFA rankings.
preview.awayForm: Japan cruised through AFC qualifying as Asia's top-ranked nation, holding 19th in the world.
preview.homeKey: Aymen Dahmen, the goalkeeper behind Tunisia's record clean-sheet run.
preview.awayKey: Takefusa Kubo, Japan's chief creative threat with Mitoma injured.
storylines: Japan must break down a defence that conceded none in qualifying
Kubo carrying extra load with Mitoma and Minamino ruled out injured
Wataru Endo anchoring Japan's midfield against Skhiri
preview.h2h: The teams last met at the 2022 Kirin Cup final, where Tunisia stunned Japan 3-0 on Japanese soil.
